picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are at home in Game 4 of the NBA Finals, leading the series 2-1, and are highly motivated after their 13-game playoff winning streak was snapped in Game 3. They boast a dominant 10-0 record in their ten playoff games prior to the Game 3 loss, showcasing their elite form and ability to win convincingly. With a healthy roster and a strong historical head-to-head advantage over the Spurs (4-1 in last 5, +9.0 ppg differential), the Knicks are poised to bounce back and cover the modest -2.5 spread at Madison Square Garden.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks
picks.rationale: Two of the three NBA Finals games between the Spurs and Knicks have gone under the 216.5 total, with scores of 200 and 209 points, indicating a trend towards tighter, more defensive contests in this high-stakes series. While Game 3 saw a higher score, the overall defensive intensity typically increases in the Finals, especially in a pivotal Game 4. Both teams are fully healthy, which often contributes to stronger defensive performances. Given the tight series and the importance of this game, a lower-scoring affair is a strong possibility.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks
picks.rationale: Victor Wembanyama is active and has demonstrated an ascending trajectory in his Points + Rebounds totals throughout the Finals, recording 36 in Game 1, 38 in Game 2, and 40 in Game 3. He delivered his best Finals performance in Game 3 with 32 points and 8 rebounds, and SportsLine projects him for 40.5 combined points and rebounds in Game 4. Given his increasing impact and the Spurs' need for his production, Wembanyama is expected to exceed this line.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks
picks.rationale: Jalen Brunson is active and has consistently been aggressive in the Finals, taking at least 25 shots in every game and averaging 27.3 points per game despite shooting below his regular-season efficiency. He scored 32 points in Game 3, demonstrating his capability to hit the 30-point mark. With the Knicks looking to rebound at home, Brunson is expected to maintain his high volume of shots and could see a positive regression in his shooting percentage, making 30+ points a valuable pick.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks
picks.rationale: Josh Hart is active and showed strong scoring in Game 3, contributing 16 points for the Knicks. He has a notable home-court scoring advantage in the playoffs, averaging almost 13 points per game at Madison Square Garden on 46% shooting. With the Knicks emphasizing more off-ball activity and Karl-Anthony Towns operating as a passer, Hart is likely to find open looks. Projections for Game 4 place his scoring between 10.8 and 12+ points, making the Over 10.5 at even money a favorable bet.

The Miami Marlins offense is currently hot, having won six of their last seven games and scoring 10 runs on 15 hits in their series opener against Arizona. Marlins' starter Ryan Gusto carries a concerning 10.80 ERA and 3.00 WHIP through his limited major league innings, suggesting he is prone to giving up runs. While Diamondbacks' starter Ryne Nelson has a 4.60 ERA, Miami's in-form lineup is capable of contributing to the total. The total line has also seen movement from 8 to 8.5, with the Over now at even money, indicating potential sharp money on a higher-scoring game.

The Atlanta Braves, with a 45-22 record, are a significantly stronger team than the 35-31 Chicago White Sox. Braves' ace Spencer Strider (8-2, 2.61 ERA, 73 K) provides a substantial pitching advantage over White Sox' Grant Holmes (4-2, 3.86 ERA). Although Ronald Acuña Jr. is day-to-day with a hamstring injury, the Braves still possess a potent lineup. The White Sox are in poor recent form, with a 2-8 record in their last 10 games and against the spread, and have several key players on the injured list. The Braves have also won four of their last five head-to-head matchups against Chicago.

The Philadelphia Phillies are in strong recent form, boasting a 7-3 record over their last ten games and a 36-31 overall standing. The Toronto Blue Jays (33-35) are hampered by an extensive injury list, including several key pitchers. While Max Scherzer is expected to return for the Blue Jays, his 9.64 ERA this season is a significant concern, giving the Phillies' starter Jesús Luzardo (4.56 ERA) a clear advantage. The Phillies also took the previous meeting in this series, demonstrating their ability to compete effectively against Toronto.

The Seattle Mariners (36-32) hold an edge over the Baltimore Orioles (31-37), who are currently on a four-game losing streak. Mariners' starter Logan Gilbert (3.79 ERA) presents a more reliable option than Orioles' Trevor Rogers, who carries a high 6.15 ERA this season. While both teams have injuries, the Orioles are missing key players like Adley Rutschman (day-to-day) and Chris Bassitt (IL). The Mariners also secured a 6-5 extra-innings victory in the previous game of this series, carrying momentum into today's matchup.

Mexico enters this World Cup opener as co-hosts with a significant home advantage at Estadio Azteca, including the challenge of high altitude for visiting teams. El Tri is in 'sensational form,' boasting an undefeated streak of six matches and a record of six wins and two draws in their last eight games, including dominant victories and draws against strong opponents. Historically, Mexico holds a 2-1-1 head-to-head record against South Africa and has scored in all four previous encounters. South Africa, in contrast, has shown turbulent form and struggled for consistency in their preparations, with key wingers Thapelo Morena and Mohau Nkota sidelined by injury.

Mexico's defense has been exceptionally strong leading up to the tournament, recording six clean sheets in their last eight matches. South Africa's offense has struggled for consistency, averaging only 0.8 goals per match in their last five head-to-head games and scoring just three goals in their last five overall matches. The only previous World Cup meeting between these two nations concluded in a 1-1 draw, suggesting a more cautious approach in such high-stakes opening games. Furthermore, predictions for this match often lean towards a low-scoring affair, with some analysts forecasting a 2-0 victory for Mexico.
